key_points: Geometry is the branch of mathematics that studies shapes, sizes and properties of space
The word geometry comes from Greek words 'geo' meaning earth and 'metrein' meaning measurement
Ancient civilizations used geometry for measuring land and constructing buildings
Euclid was a Greek mathematician known as the father of geometry
Euclid wrote a famous book called Elements which contains 13 books of geometry
Undefined terms in geometry include point, line and plane
Axioms are statements accepted as true without proof and used in mathematics
Postulates are assumptions accepted without proof and specific to geometry
Euclid proposed five postulates in geometry
Deductive reasoning is the process of deriving logical conclusions from known facts
examples: Point example: A dot marked on paper representing a location
Line example: A straight path extending infinitely in both directions
Plane example: A flat surface such as a tabletop
Axiom example: Things equal to the same thing are equal to one another
Postulate example: A straight line may be drawn joining any two points
Postulate example: A circle can be drawn with any centre and radius
formulas: Two distinct points determine exactly one straight line
exam_tips: Remember that point, line and plane are undefined terms in geometry
Axioms are universal truths while postulates apply specifically to geometry
Euclid’s Elements is one of the most important books in mathematics
Use diagrams to understand geometric concepts clearly
Revise Euclid’s postulates carefully for exam questions
